courseleaf.title: Insect Ecology & Evolution, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
The Department of Entomology & Nematology has six minor programs open to students in other disciplines seeking to complement their academic studies with focused training.

Minor Advisor

Lead Faculty Advisors

Shahid Saddique, Ph.D., Rachel L. Vannette, Ph.D., Vice Chair. 

Academic Advisor

Elvira Galvan Hack; 160 Hutchison Hall; 530-754-7277; Entomology & Nematology Advising.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
ENT 100General Entomology4
ENT 100LGeneral Entomology Laboratory 2
ENT 104Behavioral Ecology of Insects 3-4
or ENT 105 Insect Ecology
Choose at least 7 units:7-9
ENT 103
Insects Systematics
ENT 107
California Insect Diversity
ENT 109
Field Taxonomy & Ecology
ENT 158
Forensic Entomology
ESP 121Population Ecology4
or EVE 149 DISCONTINUED FOR WINTER 2026 **
Total Units20-23
**

Course(s) discontinued; see your advisor for course options.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Are you interested in the diversity, ecology, and evolution of insects? Not only are insects fascinating creatures, but they have multiple effects on the earth’s biota; including humans. The Insect Ecology & Evolution minor supports students pursuing careers in the biological sciences, wildlife management, environmental sciences, conservation, and more.
